explanation: Attributes a bad result to its cause: "because of …" with blame. It contrasts with おかげで, which credits a good result. せいか softens the claim to "perhaps because", and 〜のせいにする means to pin the blame on someone or something.
formation: N の / な-adj な / V・い-adj (plain) + せいで

examples.jp: <ruby>雨<rt>あめ</rt></ruby>のせいで、<ruby>試合<rt>しあい</rt></ruby>が<ruby>中止<rt>ちゅうし</rt></ruby>になった。
examples.en: Because of the rain, the match was called off.

examples.jp: <ruby>夜遅<rt>よるおそ</rt></ruby>くまでゲームをしたせいで、<ruby>今朝<rt>けさ</rt></ruby>は<ruby>起<rt>お</rt></ruby>きられなかった。
examples.en: Because I played games late into the night, I couldn't get up this morning.

examples.jp: <ruby>事故<rt>じこ</rt></ruby>のせいで、<ruby>電車<rt>でんしゃ</rt></ruby>が<ruby>一時間<rt>いちじかん</rt></ruby>も<ruby>遅<rt>おく</rt></ruby>れた。
examples.en: Because of the accident, the train was delayed a whole hour.
